description: Machine-readable parity contract for durable Surfer capabilities. Every fixed Surfer operation must be mapped to public SignalSurf MCP tools or explicitly listed as an exception.

operationExceptionGroups.name: database_folders
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Folder creation, rename, and delete are sidebar layout state rather than core table capability. MCP can place tables in existing folders but does not manage folder lifecycle yet.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Stable public folder vocabulary, product-scope guards, cache/realtime behavior, and tests.

operationExceptionGroups.name: surf_point_folders
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Surf Point folder operations are sidebar layout state and not core Surf Point CRUD.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Stable public folder vocabulary, product-scope guards, cache/realtime behavior, and tests.

operationExceptionGroups.name: template_and_capability_descriptions
operationExceptionGroups.reason: These are prompt/setup helpers, not durable product mutations or reads.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: A public template/catalog resource if external agents need guided setup.

operationExceptionGroups.name: item_relations
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Surfer uses the internal item_relations table and relation backfill behavior. Public MCP currently exposes schema-level relation fields and row CRUD, not item_relations CRUD/backfill.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Relation-specific scopes, product-scope guards for both endpoints, dry-run/backfill limits, audit logs, and tests.

operationExceptionGroups.name: provider_tool_setup
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Provider tool setup depends on web-session OAuth, destination pickers, provider secrets, and confirmation/audit UX.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Provider-specific scopes, OAuth destination protocol, secret redaction, confirmation model, and product-scope tests.

operationExceptionGroups.name: outbound_social_and_email
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Outbound messages and social publishing are high-risk external account actions that require provider-specific consent, previews, rate limits, and account verification.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Provider OAuth scopes, send/draft separation, preview/confirmation flow, rate limits, audit export, and sender identity checks.

operationExceptionGroups.name: bulk_and_notes
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Bulk mutations and note editing need stronger dry-run, preview, and audit guarantees than single-row public MCP CRUD.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Dry-run mode, max limits, explicit destructive scopes, confirmation semantics, and audit export.

operationExceptionGroups.name: monitoring_internals
operationExceptionGroups.reason: These operations inspect internal source quality, feed fetches, or long-running schedule state rather than stable public product records.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Stable job/source resources, schedule scopes, owner/editor policy, pause/delete semantics, and tests.

operationExceptionGroups.name: memory
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Memory may contain sensitive user and product strategy context.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Memory-specific scopes, redaction policy, deletion/export behavior, and audit logs.

operationExceptionGroups.name: inbox
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Inbox history and draft writing depend on connected accounts, contact records, and web inbox composer state.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Inbox-specific scopes, sender/account controls, draft/send separation, and audit behavior.

operationExceptionGroups.name: web_and_external_research
operationExceptionGroups.reason: General web search/fetch and social research are separate internal integrations, not SignalSurf product CRUD.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Product decision on whether SignalSurf MCP should proxy external research tools or require users to connect those MCPs separately.

operationExceptionGroups.name: surf_point_skills
operationExceptionGroups.reason: Skill attachment depends on the internal skill runtime and app-managed skill catalog.
operationExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Public skill resources, install/authorization model, and execution/audit semantics.

standaloneExceptionGroups.name: web_session_helpers
standaloneExceptionGroups.reason: These tools drive the in-app chat confirmation and OAuth destination picker UX.
standaloneExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: A public setup/confirmation protocol rather than direct MCP tool parity.

standaloneExceptionGroups.name: user_skills
standaloneExceptionGroups.reason: User skill CRUD is tied to the app-managed skill runtime and product UI.
standaloneExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Public skill resources, scopes, install policy, and audit logs.

standaloneExceptionGroups.name: deck_jobs
standaloneExceptionGroups.reason: Deck generation depends on Hermes job state, product UI state, and billing details.
standaloneExceptionGroups.requiredBeforePublic: Public job resources, polling/cancel model, billing accounting, and file delivery protocol.
